summary: Astronomical calendar from La Molina / Puruchuco — the first astronomical khipu identified from the largest excavation site (250+ khipus, 2002-2004). 225 cords, 9 STRING (4%, 89% hits), 166 INT, 50 EMPTY. Architecture 1-PAIRE, depth L4, 18 clusters. Colors: MB(159 = 71%!), AB(46), ?(8), GG(6), KB(4). Vocabulary: kaki x2 (Pleiades), pama x1 (formula), pi x1 (who?), chaka x1 (BRIDGE), waqa x1 (HUACA), mamama x1 (Moon-DIR), paqa x1 (east), maqa x1 (eclipse). 4/10 astronomical labels: kaki (Pleiades x2), mamama (Moon), maqa (eclipse), paqa (east). PLUS ceque/huaca elements: waqa (huaca) + chaka (bridge) — same vocabulary as UR203 (Pachacamac ceque registry). MB=71% dominant = category-heavy register.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  225 cords. 9 STRING (4%, 89% hits), 166 INT, 50 EMPTY.
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE, depth L4. 18 clusters.
  Colors: MB(159 = 71%), AB(46), ?(8), GG(6), KB(4) — 5 colors.
  Provenance: La Molina / Puruchuco — largest khipu excavation site.
  Museum: Puruchuco Museum (Lima).

VOCABULARY
  kaki x2 (Pleiades) — the dominant astronomical marker
  pama x1 (formula) — ritual/administrative formula
  pi x1 (who?) — interrogative
  chaka x1 (bridge) — ceque vocabulary
  waqa x1 (huaca) — sacred place
  mamama x1 (Moon-DIR) — Moon in directional case
  paqa x1 (east/dawn) — orientation
  maqa x1 (eclipse) — astronomical event

ASTRONOMICAL LABELS
  4 of 10 words are astronomical: kaki (Pleiades x2), mamama (Moon-DIR), maqa (eclipse), paqa (east). This concentration marks the document as astronomical.

CEQUE VOCABULARY
  waqa (huaca) + chaka (bridge) = the same pair that defines UR203 (Pachacamac ceque registry). An astronomical calendar anchored to the ceque system.

STRUCTURE
  L4 depth with 18 clusters — deeper and more irregular than monthly time series. MB=71% dominant = a single administrative category pervades the register. The STRING cords at L2+ levels with deep tree structure suggest a hierarchical calendar rather than a flat monthly journal.

CROSS-CORPUS
  UR165 vs UR203 (Pachacamac ceque): Both contain waqa + chaka. UR165 adds astronomical labels (kaki, mamama, maqa). UR203 is pure ceque geography.
  UR165 vs UR1145 (Pachacamac astro catalog): Both astronomical, but UR165 has ceque markers.
  UR165 vs AS159 (Ica/Pisco astro): Both have paqa (east) + maqa (eclipse). AS159 is observation-focused; UR165 integrates sacred geography.

NARRATIVE
  The Pleiades appear twice, the Moon once in its directional form — *mamama*, the Moon going somewhere — the eclipse once. But the surprise is two words that do not belong in an astronomical register: *waqa* — the huaca, the sacred place — and *chaka* — the bridge. The same pair that defines UR203, the ceque registry from Pachacamac. At Puruchuco, where two hundred and fifty khipus were excavated from a single administrative center near Lima, this document bridges the sky and the sacred landscape — an astronomical calendar anchored to the ceque system's geography of huacas and bridges.

  Nine words of string in two hundred and twenty-five cords. The register is almost entirely numerical — one hundred and sixty-six integers counted on medium-brown thread, which dominates at seventy-one percent. The scribe used one color for nearly everything, as if the entire document belonged to a single category. The astronomical words appear deep in the tree, at levels two and beyond, embedded in the hierarchical structure like annotations on a calendar. *Kaki* — the Pleiades — twice, marking the constellation that governed Inca agriculture. *Paqa* — east, dawn, the direction of first rising. *Maqa* — the eclipse, the event that demanded explanation. And *pama* — the formula, the ritual response.

  This is the first astronomical khipu identified from Puruchuco, proving the site had observatory functions alongside its known administrative role. The administrators who counted tribute and labor in this valley also counted the stars.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — Astronomical calendar with ceque vocabulary, Puruchuco provenance
Puruchuco Museum, La Molina (Lima) — excavation 2002-2004, 250+ khipus
Cross-corpus: UR203 (Pachacamac ceque registry, waqa+chaka), UR1145 (Pachacamac astro catalog), AS159 (Ica/Pisco dawn observation)
Urton, G. (2003). Signs of the Inka Khipu — Puruchuco corpus
